How to Keep Your Agentic Workflows Sustainable

AI powerhouses make money when their customers burn through as many tokens as possible, as quickly as possible. End users, on the other hand, often don't have a clear idea of how sustainable (or wasteful) their work habits are.

 

Combine these two statements, and you end up with sprawling, costly pipelines, overly complex systems, and poor ROI. Sounds familiar?

 

If you or your team want to reign in your usage, produce better results, and establish a tighter grip on resources — financial, logistical, or environmental — read on to explore this week's lineup of standout articles.

Your ReAct Agent Is Wasting 90% of Its Retries — Here’s How to Stop It 

Inefficiency and resource misuse sometimes lurk in plain sight — if you know where to look. Emmimal P Alexander presents a powerful example in a new deep dive, where she traces her benchmarking experiments focused on ReAct agents. It turns out that a few misaligned components can lead to large-scale waste; the good news is that a few careful tweaks can add robust guardrails into your pipeline.

Read More

Why Every AI Coding Assistant Needs a Memory Layer

Don't start from scratch every single time you launch a session with a coding agent. Chanin Nantasenamat explains why adding a memory layer is a crucial step towards faster and more sustainable results. 

Read More

Why AI Is Training on Its Own Garbage (and How to Fix It)

"Garbage in, garbage out" is a well-worn data science axiom, but overuse hasn't made it less true. Sabrine Bendimerad examines a promising new framework for accessing "deep web data."

Read More

Live Webinar: Modernize Your Data Pipelines for the AI Era

Most data teams know their pipelines need to evolve. Legacy batch loads and manual workflows weren't designed for the demands of real-time analytics — or the AI agents that now depend on them. When agentic RAG systems retrieve stale data, they don't just slow down; they fail in production. Join us live on April 21 to discuss what pipeline modernization actually looks like in practice.

Register Today

This Week's Most-Read Stories

In case you missed them, here are three articles that resonated with a wide audience in the past week:

  • A Visual Explanation of Linear Regression, by Mikhail Sarafanov
    How to build linear regression models, measure their quality, and improve their performance.

  • How to Run Claude Code Agents in Parallel, by Eivind Kjosbakken

    Learn how to apply coding agents in parallel to work more efficiently.

     

  • The Geometry Behind the Dot Product: Unit Vectors, Projections, and Intuition, by Amit Shreiber

    The geometric foundations you need to understand the dot product.

Other Recommended Reads

You might be interested in some of the other topics we covered recently — so we hope you explore these excellent reads:

  • How Visual-Language-Action (VLA) Models Work, by Sam Black
    The mathematical foundations of Visual-Language-Action (VLA) models for humanoid robots and more.

  • A Practical Guide to Choosing the Right Quantum SDK, by Sara A. Metwalli
    What to use, when to use it, and what to ignore?

  • Advanced RAG Retrieval: Cross-Encoders & Reranking, by Ian Ho
    A deep-dive and practical guide to cross-encoders, advanced techniques, and why your retrieval pipeline deserves a second pass.

  • How To Produce Ultra-Compact Vector Graphic Plots With Orthogonal Distance Fitting, by Ryan Burn
    Generate high-quality, minimal SVG plots by fitting Bézier curves with an ODF algorithm.

  • How Does AI Learn to See in 3D and Understand Space?, by Florent Poux
    How depth estimation, foundation segmentation, and geometric fusion are converging into spatial intelligence.

From our sister site, The New Stack

Spotify's Agentic-First Development

AI agents are changing software ownership and scale. Join this webinar for a deep dive into agentic-first development in practice and the evolution of platform engineering required to support it. 

Save Your Spot

From our partner, Octopus Deploy

AI Boosts Dev Speed — But at What Cost?

Developers write code faster, but only 1% of leaders consider their AI deployment mature. The AI Pulse report explores the real gap between AI's productivity promise and organizational outcomes.

Read the Report

Meet Our New Authors

We hope you take the time to explore top-notch work from our newest contributors:

  • Obinna Iheanachor published a compelling project walkthrough on the document-extraction system he designed.

  • Shakti Kothari devoted his debut TDS article to a hybrid marketing mix models (MMM) approach, relying on both open-source tools and generative AI.

  • Priyansh Bhardwaj shared an accessible guide to using RAG for enterprise knowledge bases.
